trans-unit.source: Projections
trans-unit.target: Projections

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: How to check EPSG-Code
trans-unit.target: How to check EPSG-Code

trans-unit.source: The default CRS/EPSG code of every QGIS project is the World Geodetic System 84 (EPSG: 4326). This CRS is optimized for world maps. So not perfect for most applications, because we mostly use maps for small areas.
trans-unit.target: The default CRS/EPSG code of every QGIS project is the World Geodetic System 84 (EPSG: 4326). This CRS is optimized for world maps. So not perfect for most applications, because we mostly use maps for small areas.

trans-unit.source: How to check EPSG-Code/CRS of your QGIS Project and change it
trans-unit.target: How to check EPSG-Code/CRS of your QGIS Project and change it

trans-unit.source: Open a QGIS project
trans-unit.target: Open a QGIS project

trans-unit.source: In the bottom right corner of QGIS you find the button `EPSG`. The number next to it is the EPSG Code currently used in the project. For more information click on the button. ![](/fig/EPSG_Code.png)
trans-unit.target: In the bottom right corner of QGIS you find the button `EPSG`. The number next to it is the EPSG Code currently used in the project. For more information click on the button. ![](/fig/EPSG_Code.png)

trans-unit.source: The window `Project Properties` will open. Here you can view all available CRS/EPSG-Code and their properties.
trans-unit.target: The window `Project Properties` will open. Here you can view all available CRS/EPSG-Code and their properties.

trans-unit.source: To change the CRS/EPSG-code, select the one you want to use and click `Apply`.
trans-unit.target: To change the CRS/EPSG-code, select the one you want to use and click `Apply`.

trans-unit.source: How to check EPSG-Code/CRS of layer/ data
trans-unit.target: How to check EPSG-Code/CRS of layer/ data

trans-unit.source: Right click on the data layer, click on `Properties`.
trans-unit.target: Right click on the data layer, click on `Properties`.

trans-unit.source: The “Layer Properties” Window of the data layer will open. Click on `Information`.
trans-unit.target: The “Layer Properties” Window of the data layer will open. Click on `Information`.

trans-unit.source: Under the headline “Coordinate Reference System (CRS)” you find all information about the CRS. The most important are:
trans-unit.target: Under the headline “Coordinate Reference System (CRS)” you find all information about the CRS. The most important are:

trans-unit.source: __Name:__ Here you find the EPSG Code.
trans-unit.target: __Name:__ Here you find the EPSG Code.

trans-unit.source: __Unites:__ Here you can find whether it is possible to use meters with this data layer or latitude and longitude.
trans-unit.target: __Unites:__ Here you can find whether it is possible to use meters with this data layer or latitude and longitude.

trans-unit.source: Changing the projection of a vector layer
trans-unit.target: Changing the projection of a vector layer

trans-unit.source: `Vector` Tab → `Data Management Tools` → `Reproject Layer`.
trans-unit.target: `Vector` Tab → `Data Management Tools` → `Reproject Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Select target CRS/ EPSG-Code.
trans-unit.target: Select target CRS/ EPSG-Code.

trans-unit.source: Save the new file by clicking on the three dots next to `Reprojected`, specify the file name and the location where you want to save the file.
trans-unit.target: Save the new file by clicking on the three dots next to `Reprojected`, specify the file name and the location where you want to save the file.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Changing the projection of a raster layer
trans-unit.target: Changing the projection of a raster layer

trans-unit.source: `Raster` Tab → `Projections` → `Warp (Reproject)`.
trans-unit.target: `Raster` Tab → `Projections` → `Warp (Reproject)`.

trans-unit.source: Select target CRS/EPSG-Code.
trans-unit.target: Select target CRS/EPSG-Code.

trans-unit.source: Select resampling method.
trans-unit.target: Select resampling method.

trans-unit.source: Save the new file by clicking on the three dots next to `Reprojected`, specify the file name and the location where you want to save the file.
trans-unit.target: Save the new file by clicking on the three dots next to `Reprojected`, specify the file name and the location where you want to save the file.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: Common mistakes with Coordinate Reference Systems
trans-unit.target: Common mistakes with Coordinate Reference Systems

trans-unit.source: The website [__I Hate Coordinate Systems!__](https://ihatecoordinatesystems.com/) offers a “a problem-based guide of common CRS issues, root causes, and solutions.”.
trans-unit.target: The website [__I Hate Coordinate Systems!__](https://ihatecoordinatesystems.com/) offers a “a problem-based guide of common CRS issues, root causes, and solutions.”.
